response.justification: This candidate has strong software craftsmanship fundamentals and proven ability to work on complex systems, but lacks the core technical expertise the role demands. There is no evidence of Java, Kotlin, or Spring ecosystem experience, which are required qualifications. The last meaningful Java work was at Altom (2016–2018, now 8 years ago), and the candidate's recent trajectory (current role focused on TypeScript, Node.js, and AI-agent infrastructure) moves away from the JVM ecosystem entirely. The role requires deep expertise in enterprise Java frameworks and IDE internals; this candidate's generalist approach and lack of domain specialization create a significant gap.
strengths: Demonstrates strong software craftsmanship discipline: TDD, clean architecture, and focus on testability across multiple projects
Proven ability to design and own complex systems at scale (microservices, geo-data platforms, large-scale data pipelines)
Excellent communication and mentoring skills, with track record of teaching workshops, organizing coding dojos, and contributing to open-source communities
concerns: No Java or Kotlin expertise demonstrated in the resume; last Java work was 8 years ago at Altom and mentioned only in passing alongside D
No experience with Spring, Hibernate, Quarkus, or any enterprise Java frameworks—explicit required qualifications
No background in IDE internals or language tooling; only legacy experience with National Instruments LabWindows/CVI (2014–2015), leaving a significant gap in this critical focus area

key_factors.factor: Spring ecosystem and enterprise framework experience
